dataset: ICD-10-WHO (World Health Organization International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision)

pi_items.source: WHO ICD-10 official classification; verified via ICD10data.com, gesund.bund.de, AAPC ICD-10 codes database
WHO ICD-10 official classification for code A00; verified via multiple ICD-10 medical coding databases
OpenMRS/Bahmni documentation and community forums confirm ICD-10-WHO is the standard concept source name for ICD-10 codes in OpenMRS installations

WHO official cholera fact sheet and CDC Yellow Book definition: 'An acute diarrheal infection caused by consuming food or water contaminated with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae'. All four keywords appear in official medical literature and the task description.
Standard OpenMRS admin path is /openmrs/admin. The localhost URL is appropriate for local deployments as shown in task setup scripts.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified against authoritative sources. ICD-10 code A00 is the official WHO classification for Cholera, an acute diarrheal infection caused by Vibrio cholerae bacterium. Code A00 and name 'Cholera' are standard medical classifications from WHO/ICD-10. ICD-10-WHO is a standard OpenMRS concept source name used for ICD-10 codes. The admin URL follows OpenMRS standard paths. The medical description in the task matches WHO official definitions and includes all required keywords (acute, diarrhoeal, vibrio, cholerae).
